title: DevOps BizOps Engineer
description: Job Description* Job Title: DevOps BizOps Engineer City: Dublin State/Province: Dublin Posting Start Date: 4/7/26 Wipro Limited (NYSE: WIT, BSE: 507685, NSE: WIPRO) is a leading technology services and consulting company focused on building innovative solutions that address clients’ most complex digital transformation needs. Leveraging our holistic portfolio of capabilities in consulting, design, engineering, and operations, we help clients realize their boldest ambitions and build future\-ready, sustainable businesses. With over 230,000 employees and business partners across 65 countries, we deliver on the promise of helping our customers, colleagues, and communities thrive in an ever\-changing world. For additional information, visit us at www.wipro.com. Job Description: **Job Description** ------------------- As a BizOps Senior Engineer, you will act as the production readiness steward for versatile Gateway products and integration with other platforms. You will partner with development teams to design, implement, and support services with a focus on operational resilience, automation, and compliance. Should have knowledge of Payment domains specially in Gateways with least gRPC experience and strong with SRE fundamentals and hands\-on experience **Key Responsibilities** * Lifecycle Ownership: Engage in and improve the entire service lifecycle—from design and deployment to operations and continuous improvement. * Operational Readiness: Ensure system availability, capacity, performance, monitoring, and self\-healing capabilities are embedded throughout delivery. * Incident Management: Practice sustainable incident response, lead blameless postmortems, and optimize Mean Time to Recovery (MTTR). * Develop and maintain automation pipelines for certificate renewal, traffic routing, alerting, and compliance reporting using tools like Ansible, Venafi \& XLR template. * Support CI/CD pipelines for software promotion and operational gating. * Reliability Engineering: Scale systems sustainably through automation and advocate for changes that improve reliability and velocity. * Compliance \& Risk Management: Drive initiatives for Safety \& Soundness, PCI compliance, threat/toil reduction, and ITSM defect resolution. * Monitoring \& Observability: Implement robust logging, monitoring, and alerting standards to ensure system health and proactive issue detection. Hands\-on experience with Dynatrace \& Splunk monitoring tool configuration and alerting. * Collaboration: Work with global teams across multiple time zones and mentor junior engineers. Continuous Improvement: Provide feedback loops to development teams on resiliency gaps and operational enhancements. * **Required Experience \& Skills** * Experience: Extensive experience in BizOps, Site Reliability Engineering, or DevOps roles. * Strong understanding of NGINX configuration and gRPC event\-driven architectures. * Proficiency in DevOps tools: Chef, Jenkins, Groovy, shell scripting, Bitbucket, Git, Ansible, XLR. * Experience with AWS infrastructure, secure access practices, and cloud\-native deployments. * Security \& Compliance: * Awareness of certificate lifecycle management, mutual TLS, SSL handshake, SSH keys, encryption standards. * Familiarity with ITSM processes, compliance frameworks, and incident management. * Networking \& Systems: * Knowledge of client\-server relationships, network layers (L1–L7\), load balancers (BIG\-IP F5\), and application firewalls. * Ability to analyze stack traces, TCP dumps, heap/thread dumps, and perform OS\-level troubleshooting. * Authentication \& Authorization: * Intermediate understanding of Active Directory, SAML, LTPA, SSO, OAuth. **͏** ----- **Deliver** **No** **Performance Parameter** **Measure** 1\. Continuous Integration,Deployment \& Monitoring 100% error free on boarding \& implementation 2\. title: Eirdata Graduate Engineer
description: **Who we are;** **Established in 2001, Eirdata are specialists in cleanroom validation, commissioning \& compliance. We provide these range of services to the pharmaceutical, medical devices and healthcare sectors primarily. In 2013, Eirdata was acquired by ESS Group.** **ESS Group includes three companies – Engineering Support \& Services Ltd. (ESS), Eirdata Environmental Services Ltd. (Eirdata), and the newly joined Critical Airflow Europe (CAE).*** **ESS are experts in asset management and maintenance reliability through people.** * **Eirdata are specialists in cleanroom validation, commissioning and compliance, HVAC systems, indoor air quality and building wellness.** * **CAE are the European leader in delivering safe energy efficient laboratory control and critical airflow applications. Critical Airflow Europe Ltd represents Phoenix, Strobic and Aircuity.** **Summary of the Role** The aim of the Graduate Programme is to develop the best and brightest engineering talent to become the future leaders. Within Eirdata you will be part of a multi\-skilled and adaptable team, ready and prepared to take on a wide variety of technical challenges throughout various industries. All our structured graduate programmes provide a collaborative, open environment where graduates have a voice to bring their own great ideas in the business. The programme also supports graduates in developing real\-world skills such as project management, communication, and technical role\-specific skills, among others. **Our Goal** * Establish a flexible and multi\-skilled workforce capable of evolving the business landscape. * Develop, motivate, and retain our graduates. * Maximise the knowledge and experience within our company and ensure that this knowledge is captured and updated effectively. * Provide skills for future innovations within Eirdata. **Duties \& Main Responsibilities*** Commissioning, troubleshooting, testing and validation of HVAC systems including GxP HVAC Systems, LEV’s and Fume Cupboards and associated utilities systems. * HEPA Filter Integrity Testing * Particle Count testing * Verification of cleanroom air change rates and Cleanroom pressure profiles * HVAC Air and Water Systems Balancing and Commissioning * HVAC Systems Trouble Shooting and Fault Finding * Complete validation documentation, check out sheets and complete detailed HVAC commissioning reports. * Technical support * Good Documentation Practices (Knowledge of ALCOA\+ principles) **Other Duties as part of the team** * Energy \- Survey and implementation of energy saving projects. * External audit preparation and support. * New installations, modifications, and improvements to existing systems. **Education \& Qualifications*** Relevant Engineering Degree L7/8 \- Mechanical/Electrical/Building Services * Excellent communication skills both written and verbal. * Computer Literate. * Ability to work on own initiative. **Required Skills** * Working knowledge of Microsoft office (excel, word, PowerPoint). * Team player, with a flexible approach but also able to work under own initiative. * Strong verbal and interpersonal skills * Competent level of technical writing and understanding of technical documentation. * Full clean driving licence required. * Must be eligible to work in Ireland. **Benefits** Eirdata offer support that enables you to never stop growing. Your reward at Eirdata is not just your salary, we believe in investing in our people's personal, professional development and empowering their work\-life balance to enable continuous growth. We offer supports and benefits that suit you, wherever you are in life, including Health care, pension, training, coaching. * Competitive Salay * Very attractive pension scheme * VHI * Laptop * Phone * Paid Annual Leave * Supporting your work life balance

title: Electrical Estimator
description: **TDE Group – Electrical Contractors** Kilcullen, Co. Kildare TDE Group are an expanding electrical contractor delivering commercial, industrial and fit\-out projects across Ireland. Our team has grown rapidly in recent years and we are now seeking an Electrical Estimator / Junior Estimator to support the preparation of electrical tenders and pricing of upcoming projects. Our recent work includes warehouse and logistics projects, commercial fit\-outs across Ireland. This is an excellent opportunity for someone with an electrical trade background or construction estimating experience to develop their career within a growing company. Key Responsibilities * Review tender drawings, specifications and project documentation to understand project scope and requirements. * Carry out detailed quantity take\-offs from electrical drawings including lighting, power, containment, distribution, fire alarm and specialist systems. * Assist in preparing detailed cost estimates including labour, materials, plant and subcontract packages. * Issue supplier and subcontractor enquiries, ensuring competitive quotations are obtained within tender deadlines. * Analyse supplier quotations and ensure all items are compliant with the project specification and scope. * Prepare Bills of Quantities and pricing schedules for tender submissions. * Assist in compiling complete tender submissions, including pricing breakdowns, clarifications and supporting documentation. * Maintain and develop an internal cost database including labour rates, material pricing and historical project costs. * Liaise with suppliers, subcontractors and manufacturers to obtain technical and commercial information required for tenders. * Attend site visits and tender walkdowns where required to assess installation conditions and logistics. * Work with the directors to review tender risk, programme durations and commercial considerations. * Support post\-tender clarifications and negotiations with clients or main contractors. * Assist with project handover to the delivery team once contracts are awarded. * Maintain accurate records of tender submissions, pricing information and supplier quotations Requirements * Electrical trade background or construction related qualification preferred * Ability to read and interpret electrical drawings * Strong organisational and communication skills * Good knowledge of Microsoft Excel and Office * Attention to detail and ability to meet deadlines * Full driving licence What We Offer * Salary €35,000 – €45,000 depending on experience * Opportunity to progress into a senior estimating role * Exposure to commercial and industrial electrical projects * A supportive working environment within a growing contractor * Location: Kilcullen, Co. Kildare * Apply by sending CV to: Info@TDEGroup.ie Job Type: Full\-time Pay: €35,000\.00\-€45,000\.00 per year Work Location: In person

title: Employment Associate
description: **Employment Solicitor (1\-4 PQE) \| Top Tier Law Firm \| Dublin** We are currently working with a top tier Dublin law firm seeking to hire a talented Employment Solicitor (0\-4 PQE) to join its highly regarded and growing team. This is an excellent opportunity for a junior to mid\-level solicitor to develop their career within a market\-leading practice advising a high\-profile domestic and international client base. The Role This team advises on a broad range of employment law matters, both contentious and non\-contentious, including: * Employment contracts, policies, and HR advisory work * Workplace investigations and disciplinary processes * Employment aspects of corporate transactions (M\&A support) * Litigation before the WRC, Labour Court, and civil courts * Redundancy and restructuring projects * Equality and regulatory matters You will work closely with experienced partners on complex, high\-value matters and gain exposure to top\-tier clients across a variety of sectors. The Candidate The ideal candidate will have: * 1\-4 years' post\-qualification experience in employment law * Experience from a leading firm * Excellent technical ability and attention to detail * Strong communication and interpersonal skills * A genuine interest in developing a long\-term career in employment law **\*\*\* Be admitted as a Solicitor in Ireland** The Opportunity This firm offers: * Exposure to some of the most high\-profile employment work in the Irish market * A supportive and collaborative team environment * Excellent training and career progression opportunities * Competitive remuneration and benefits package * Hybrid working model This is a fantastic opportunity to join a top tier firm at an exciting stage of growth. **For more information or a confidential discussion, please get in touch briona.brogan@robertwalters.com.** **Robert Walters Operations Limited is an employment business and employment agency and welcomes applications from all candidates**

title: FP&A Analyst
description: **We are partnering with a major national energy organisation to appoint an FP\&A Analyst. This is a key business‑facing role that blends financial modelling, forecasting, performance analysis, and cross‑functional stakeholder engagement.** **The position offers strong exposure to senior management, ownership of budgeting and planning cycles, and the opportunity to influence decision‑making through high‑quality financial insight and commentary.** **Responsibilities:** * Leading the budgeting, forecasting, and long‑term planning processes, ensuring timely and accurate submissions. * Partnering with business units to understand financial drivers, identify risks and opportunities, and support commercial decision‑making. * Analysing monthly performance, preparing clear variance analysis, insights, and presentations for senior stakeholders. * Developing and enhancing financial models, dashboards, and analytical tools to improve visibility and accuracy. * Driving continuous improvement and supporting automation across FP\&A processes. * Ensuring strong governance, control compliance, and alignment with audit standards. **Requirements:** * Qualified accountant (ACA, ACCA, CIMA) with at least 2 years’ post‑qualification experience. * Excellent financial, analytical, and problem‑solving skills. * Strong Excel capability with experience in data‑driven analysis; Power BI or similar tools an advantage. * Confident communicator with the ability to engage and challenge senior stakeholders effectively. * Highly organised, detail‑oriented, and comfortable managing competing priorities. * Experience with budgeting, forecasting, or financial modelling beneficial; familiarity with SAP or other planning systems a plus. **Our client offers a hybrid working environment (minimum two days on‑site), flexible working arrangements, and the opportunity to contribute to Ireland’s transition to a net‑zero energy system.** Apply directly via the link provided or contact Matthew Fleming at mfleming@elevatepartners.ie.

title: FP&A Analyst
description: A leading global investment manager is building out its Finance team and this role sits right at the centre of it. This is not a back\-office reporting role. It’s a commercial, forward\-looking FP\&A seat with real visibility to senior leadership and direct involvement in decision making. **The Role** * Own monthly financial dashboards for senior management * Analyse revenue, cost drivers and KPIs to improve forecasting accuracy * Partner with business leaders on budgeting and strategic initiatives * Build financial models to support product expansion and growth decisions * Work cross\-functionally with Risk, Strategy and HR on firm wide projects * Support financial reporting as required **The Opportunity** * Newly created team in Dublin \- genuine opportunity to shape and influence * Direct exposure to global FP\&A teams * Strong blend of commercial FP\&A \+ technical finance / reporting * High visibility role with interaction at senior stakeholder level * Fast\-paced, high\-performance environment where strong analysts thrive **Requirements** * 4\+ years FP\&A experience (financial services preferred) * Strong Excel (VBA / macros a plus) and analytical skillset * Ability to communicate clearly and influence stakeholders * Comfortable operating in a fast\-moving, high\-pressure environment

title: Finance Associate
description: **We are supporting a leading global investment firm to appoint a Finance Associate / Senior Finance Associate.** **This role is ideal for Big‑4 professionals seeking their first move into industry, or someone with 1\-2 years' PQE offering exposure to complex investment structures, financial reporting, and cross‑functional collaboration within a sophisticated alternatives platform.** **Responsibilities:** * Support senior finance leadership across a range of fund‑level reporting and operational finance activities. * Review investment structures and assess accounting, tax, and regulatory considerations. * Assist with investor reporting, fund performance analysis, and ad‑hoc information requests. * Contribute to the preparation and review of quarterly and annual financial statements. * Coordinate audit processes and liaise with external advisors. * Support reviews of valuations, capital activity, and fee calculations. **Requirements:** * Newly Qualified Accountant from Big‑4 audit. (FS audit, asset management, banking, or structured finance advantageous). Or 1\-2 years' PQE in funds. * Strong technical grounding in accounting, financial reporting, and controls. * Excellent analytical skills and strong attention to detail. * Comfortable interpreting complex financial documentation. * Confident communicator with the ability to work collaboratively across teams. * Advanced Excel skills; professional qualification (ACA/ACCA/CPA) required or nearing completion. **This is a great opportunity to step directly into the finance team of a global investment organisation, gaining exposure to alternative investment strategies, sophisticated fund structures, and a high‑performance international environment. The role is Dublin‑based with hybrid working and strong long‑term career progression.** Apply in confidence or contact Matthew Fleming at mfleming@elevatepartners.ie.

title: Financial Analyst
description: **We are seeking a Financial Analyst to join a growing organisation operating within the energy and infrastructure sector, to support a portfolio of large\-scale projects. The business is operating in a capital\-intensive environment and continues to expand, offering strong exposure across project delivery, commercial decision\-making, and senior stakeholder engagement.** **Key Responsibilities:** * Partner with project and senior stakeholders to provide financial insight, challenge assumptions, and support decision\-making. * Support budgeting, forecasting, and business case development, including scenario analysis and cost evaluation. * Prepare and review monthly management accounts, including variance analysis and clear performance commentary. * Maintain strong financial controls and support audit, compliance, and governance requirements across projects. * Contribute to financial planning, cashflow forecasting, and the ongoing development of finance processes. **Key Requirements:** * Qualified accountant (ACA / ACCA / CIMA or equivalent) with approximately 1–3 years’ post\-qualification experience. * Experience in a finance business partnering or commercially focused finance role. * Background in a capital\-intensive environment such as energy, infrastructure, construction, or property. * Strong experience in management accounting, budgeting, forecasting, and financial analysis. * Confident communicator with the ability to engage and challenge non\-finance stakeholders. **This role offers the opportunity to develop business partnering experience within a commercial finance environment, supporting key projects within a Joint Venture alongside major industry organisations.** For a confidential discussion, please contact Matthew Fleming at mfleming@elevatepartners.ie.

title: From Events to Career Progression – Get into Recruitment!
description: **From Events to Career Progression – Get into Recruitment!** * **You’ve proven you can handle pressure — now prove how far you can go** * **Stop resetting after every event — start building long\-term progression** * **Your next role shouldn’t be “another job” — it should be a step up** We’re Access, one of Ireland’s fastest\-growing recruitment companies — and every single leader in our business started in an entry\-level role . **Progression here isn’t a promise. It’s a pattern** . In events, you deliver, reset, and start again, with no clear long\-term progression. In recruitment, your determination builds over time: you’ll own relationships, develop your market, and progress based on performance *. Hannah joined us with no recruitment experience and was promoted to Director just last month, showing exactly what happens when ambition meets a structure designed for growth* . **What’s in it for you?** * **Clear promotion pathway** – you’ll always know what’s needed to reach the next level, giving you control over your progression * **Fast\-track progression** – you’ll be able to move quickly when you perform, without waiting years for opportunities * **Leadership opportunities** – you’ll have the chance to step into management roles and increase your impact * **Increasing responsibility and autonomy** – you’ll grow into a role where your decisions shape outcomes * **A career that builds over time** – you’ll stop starting from zero and start creating long\-term success **What we look for:** * 1 to 3 years of experience as an **Event Coordinator, Event Executive, Project Coordinator, Account Executive, or similar** * Experience working in fast\-paced, deadline\-driven environments * Strong stakeholder management and organisational skills * Ambition to progress and take on more responsibility **Additional Information:** Full\-time, office\-based role in Dublin City Centre, Monday to Friday. In your first 2 years, you can expect to earn an average of €64K\+ OTE, with accelerated progression based on performance. For more information, contact Ines Duarte confidentially at \+353 1 572 118 1 or email ines.duarte@accesshealthcare.ie

title: Graduate Building Services Engineer (Dublin) 2026
description: **Overview** **The Opportunity** Get started on an exciting and meaningful career path, working on innovative, challenging projects and learning from industry experts as part of PM Group’s world leading multi\-discipline team. **Who are PM Group?** PM Group is a leading International engineering, architecture, project and construction management firm. With a network of offices in Europe, Asia and the US and a multi\-discipline team of 3,800 people, we deliver complex, capital projects in the life sciences, food and beverages, mission critical/ICT, advanced manufacturing, energy and environmental sectors. We are 100% employee owned so work together to make our company successful. **Responsibilities** As a Building Services Graduate at PM Group, your day to day role would involve, but would not be limited to the following activities: * Work on exciting projects in the Mission Critical, Pharma, Med Tech, Food, Advance Manufacturing and Energy sectors * Coordination with clients, contractors, vendors and other PM Group departments / offices with regard to scope management , deliverables, time lines, resources and 3rd party interfaces / queries. This could include: * BCAR inspections * Support during construction (contractor queries and submittals) * Assist with commissioning activities * Coordination of design with other disciplines * In conjunction with senior engineers and designers preparation of the following deliverables at various project stages: * Calculations (Heating, Cooling, Utilities, etc) and Utility Load Analysis * Schematics (Air \& Instrument Diagrams, Utility Process \& Instrument Diagrams) * Schedules \& Lists (Equipment, Inline and Terminal items, etc) * Data Sheets (AHUs, Boilers, Chillers, etc) * Drawings (AHU Zoning, Pressurisation, Services General Arrangements) * Pipe Stress Analysis and Fluid Flow modelling * Contract Requisitions (installation contracts, turnkey equipment) * Cost Estimates **Qualifications** * Minimum qualification: level 8 degree, level 9 preferred in Building Services or Mechanical Engineering * Ability to work in a vibrant multi discipline collaborative design environment * Excellent interpersonal and communication skills * Strong work ethic and self\-starter * Flexibility to work both in a design office and on a construction site **What are we offering?** An exciting and meaningful career path, working on innovative, challenging projects and learning from industry experts as part of PM Group’s world leading multi\-discipline team. We offer a competitive salary with opportunities to travel and gain a variety of experiences. We also offer an education assisstance programme for anyone interested in furthering their educationch and chartership routes. It's not all work. At PM Group we work hard, but we make sure to have fun too! Be part of the bigger picture and apply today.

title: Graduate Programme 2026
description: **Windward are now accepting applications for their brand\-new Graduate Management Programme commencing in September 2026\.** The successful applicants will each be placed into a Graduate Manager role in one of our managed hotels across Ireland including ***Farnham Estate Spa \& Golf Resort, Aghadoe Heights Hotel \& Spa, Mount Wolseley Hotel Spa \& Golf Resort, The Diamond Coast Hotel, Connemara Coast Hotel and Harvey’s Point Lakeside Resort.*** **About us** Windward are a team of highly experienced hotel operators and investors with a hotel portfolio of approx. 2,000 keys\* in Ireland and Continental Europe. Founded in 2007, Windward is a leading hospitality management company, managing and operating hotels on behalf of their owners. **Requirements** * 3rd level degree in Hotel or Hospitality Management or related field of study * Willingness to relocate to location across Ireland * Passionate about hotels and the hospitality industry Applications are now open so apply today! **About The Role** **The Programme** Commencing in September 2026, the new Windward Graduate Management Programme is an 18\-month, rotational programme designed to equip graduates with the skills, knowledge and experience necessary to become future leaders in the hospitality industry by providing comprehensive training and hands\-on experience across various departments within our hotels. The programme is structured into six 3\-month rotations across different departments across: * Front Office * Reservations \& Revenue Management * Food \& Beverage * Housekeeping * Support Functions (Finance, HR, Sales \& Marketing) * Ops Management This rotational approach allows graduates to gain in\-depth experience and an understanding of each department's operations and how they interconnect to deliver exceptional guest experiences. In addition to the day\-to\-day experience, Graduate Managers will be enrolled on numerous additional qualifications to enhance their professional and personal development including Management Training Level 5, Train the Trainer and much more. With guidance from General Managers and HR, the programme will be underpinned by a curriculum of learning objectives that the graduate must achieve to ensure they are on the right track. Each graduate will also be assigned a mentor within the hotel to provide support and guidance. The overall objective of the programme is to create a pipeline to secure and train excellent future managers and leaders across the group. **Required Criteria** **Skills Needed** **About The Company** Since its inception in 2007, Windward Management Limited has built a reputation for excellence in hotel management. With headquarters in Dublin, the company provides tailored solutions for hotel owners, including operations, marketing, and financial management. Windward's team is driven by a passion for delivering exceptional guest experiences while ensuring each property achieves its full potential. Recognized for its dedication to innovation and operational success, the company is equally committed to investing in its people. Professionals joining Windward Management will become part of a supportive and dynamic environment, with abundant opportunities to grow and excel within a thriving sector. **Company Culture** At Windward Management, people are at the heart of success. The company nurtures a culture rooted in respect, collaboration, and a shared commitment to excellence. Employees are empowered to innovate and contribute to meaningful outcomes while enjoying a strong focus on work\-life balance. Windward fosters professional growth through tailored development opportunities, ensuring each team member can achieve their career aspirations. This positive, inclusive workplace values individual contributions, encourages open communication, and celebrates success collectively. For hospitality professionals seeking a rewarding career in an environment where their efforts truly matter, Windward Management offers an exceptional opportunity to thrive. **Company Benefits** Windward Management Limited understands the value of its people and offers a benefits package designed to support and reward its employees. Competitive salaries are complemented by a supportive work environment that prioritizes work\-life balance and career development. Team members benefit from opportunities to learn, grow, and advance in their careers, with access to mentoring and professional training programs. title: Graduate Programme 2026
description: **Job Description** **Location:** Windward Management Limited, 19/20 Fleet Street **Windward are now accepting applications for their brand\-new Graduate Management Programme commencing in September 2026\.** The successful applicants will each be placed into a Graduate Manager role in one of our managed hotels across Ireland including ***Farnham Estate Spa \& Golf Resort, Aghadoe Heights Hotel \& Spa, Mount Wolseley Hotel Spa \& Golf Resort, The Diamond Coast Hotel, Connemara Coast Hotel and Harvey’s Point Lakeside Resort.*** **About Us** Windward are a team of highly experienced hotel operators and investors with a hotel portfolio of approx. 2,000 keys\* in Ireland and Continental Europe. Founded in 2007, Windward is a leading hospitality management company, managing and operating hotels on behalf of their owners. **Requirements** * 3rd level degree in Hotel or Hospitality Management or related field of study * Willingness to relocate to location across Ireland * Passionate about hotels and the hospitality industry Applications are now open so apply today! **About The Role** **The Programme** Commencing in September 2026, the new Windward Graduate Management Programme is an 18\-month, rotational programme designed to equip graduates with the skills, knowledge and experience necessary to become future leaders in the hospitality industry by providing comprehensive training and hands\-on experience across various departments within our hotels. The programme is structured into six 3\-month rotations across different departments across: * Front Office * Reservations \& Revenue Management * Food \& Beverage * Housekeeping * Support Functions (Finance, HR, Sales \& Marketing) * Ops Management This rotational approach allows graduates to gain in\-depth experience and an understanding of each department's operations and how they interconnect to deliver exceptional guest experiences. In addition to the day\-to\-day experience, Graduate Managers will be enrolled on numerous additional qualifications to enhance their professional and personal development including Management Training Level 5, Train the Trainer and much more. With guidance from General Managers and HR, the programme will be underpinned by a curriculum of learning objectives that the graduate must achieve to ensure they are on the right track. Each graduate will also be assigned a mentor within the hotel to provide support and guidance. The overall objective of the programme is to create a pipeline to secure and train excellent future managers and leaders across the group. **Skills Needed** **About The Company** Since its inception in 2007, Windward Management Limited has built a reputation for excellence in hotel management. With headquarters in Dublin, the company provides tailored solutions for hotel owners, including operations, marketing, and financial management. Windward's team is driven by a passion for delivering exceptional guest experiences while ensuring each property achieves its full potential. Recognized for its dedication to innovation and operational success, the company is equally committed to investing in its people. Professionals joining Windward Management will become part of a supportive and dynamic environment, with abundant opportunities to grow and excel within a thriving sector. **Company Culture** At Windward Management, people are at the heart of success. The company nurtures a culture rooted in respect, collaboration, and a shared commitment to excellence. Employees are empowered to innovate and contribute to meaningful outcomes while enjoying a strong focus on work\-life balance. Windward fosters professional growth through tailored development opportunities, ensuring each team member can achieve their career aspirations. This positive, inclusive workplace values individual contributions, encourages open communication, and celebrates success collectively. For hospitality professionals seeking a rewarding career in an environment where their efforts truly matter, Windward Management offers an exceptional opportunity to thrive. **Required Criteria** **Desired Criteria** **Closing Date** Sunday 31st May, 2026 **Contract Type** fulltime **Salary** Based on Experience

title: Health and Safety Teacher evenings online @CMI College
description: **Occupational Health \& Safety Teacher part time, online** CMI is expanding and offering an exciting opportunity to join our teaching team and work as a part time teacher on our professional Occupational Health \& Safety Diploma and Certificate qualifications. This is a part time role that is ideal for experienced health and safety practitioners who can devote one to two evenings a week to teach online. All our new teachers receive full teacher and assessor training and mentoring. A comprehensive range of quality teaching materials and resources is provided . The ideal candidate must enjoy teaching/training and sharing their knowledge. Eligible candidates should check they meet the eligibility criteria listed for this role. Classes are held through live online classes through the CMI learning platform and are held one \- two nights per week on Tuesday, Weds and Thursday evening, times 18:30\-21:30\. The units cover: * Health \& Safety principles * Risk Management * Legal and Regulation * Maintaining a Safety culture Eligibility Criteria * 10 \+ years experience as a Health \& Safety practitioner or Manager. * A relevant Degree/ or Masters qualification is required in Occupational Health \& Safety. * Graduate or Chartered Member of IOSH preferred * Copies of health and safety qualifications will be required * 1\-2 years prior teaching experience desirable, not mandatory * Candidates must have fast broadband either in their home or workplace (more than 5Mps upload and download). This is an exciting and prestigious role to work with a dynamic education institute over 3 x 10 week terms every year. Job Type: Part\-time Pay: €44\.00 per teaching hour Expected hours: 3 \-6 hours per week

title: Healthcare Assistant - Beaumont (Full Time)
description: **We are currently recruiting for Full\-Time Health Care Assistants for Beaumont Lodge, Kilmore Road, Artane, Dublin 5\.** **Hours of work:** 39 hours p/w **Salary Range:** €32,691 per annum **Responsibilities:** * To work as part of a team of Health Care Assistants, under the direction of the Nursing team * To provide quality person\-centred care to meet the needs of residents/patients * To provide high standard services as specified in agreed care plans and to contribute to the continual review of resident’s needs * Enable and assist resident to maintain their personal hygiene and appearance * Enable and assist resident to eat and drink * Enable and assist resident to access and use toilet facilities, participating in individual continence promotion programmes * Contribute to the movement and treatment of resident to maximise their physical comfort * Promote mobility of resident, supervising and assisting as necessary to ensure resident retains optimum mobility * Maintain high level of communication between resident’s family and staff developing good relationships * To work closely with the activity co\-ordinator/social care leader of the Residential home and ensure engagement of the residents at arranged activities where possible and suitable. * To escort/Accompany residents to the hospital or for other appointments when directed by the senior nursing staff. * Staff adhere to infection control and prevention policy * Safekeeping of resident’s personal property * Report broken or unsafe items that need repair * Assess and record vital signs and report any concerns to nursing team * Assess and record weights and MUST scores and report any concerns to nursing team * Participate in CPR and basic lifesaving and act as a first responder for the HCA team * Takes blood samples from residents/patients following phlebotomy training **The ideal candidate should have the following attributes:** Cheerful and compassionate by nature, you will possess sound judgement, initiative and problem\-solving abilities and be able to pay attention to the details. Reading, writing and communicating clearly in English is vital. Experience is preferred, but not as essential as your genuine desire to develop or progress a career in this field is a vital attribute. A respectful and courteous attitude to seniors is required, and weekend availability is highly desirable. **Qualifications:** QQI Level 5 in Healthcare Support or Equivalent **Benefits:** * HSF Health Plan * Education Support * Employee Assistant Programme * Length of Service Reward * Birthday Reward * Subsidised Meals * Bike to Work Scheme **Bartra Healthcare is an equal opportunities employer** Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent Pay: €32,691\.00 per year Benefits: * On\-site parking Application question(s): * Do have the QQI Level 5 in Healthcare Support? Education: * Junior Certificate (preferred) Work Location: In person

title: IT Risk + Controls Analyst
description: IT Risk Controls Analyst **Permanent \- Dublin West \- Hybrid (1 day on site per week)** The Role We are seeking an IT Risk Controls Analyst to join a growing team within a global technology\-focused organisation. This position offers the opportunity to play a key role in strengthening IT risk and control frameworks within a fast\-paced, data\-driven environment. Key Responsibilities * Design, implement, and maintain IT security controls aligned with industry standards * Support and enhance third\-party risk management frameworks and processes * Assess, monitor, and mitigate IT and cybersecurity risks across the organisation * Ensure alignment with relevant regulatory and compliance frameworks * Collaborate with internal stakeholders to improve the overall control environment * Contribute to continuous improvement of governance, risk, and compliance (GRC) practices Skills \& Experience * Proven experience in designing and implementing security controls * Strong knowledge of third\-party risk management * Familiarity with regulatory frameworks such as ISO 27001, DORA, and NIST 2 * Experience working within IT risk, controls, or cybersecurity functions * Strong analytical, problem\-solving, and communication skills * Exposure to ISO 42001 is advantageous but not essential

title: Immigration & Asylum Law Lecturer
description: **Lecturer \- Immigration \& Asylum Law** Diversity and inclusivity are core values of the City College Dublin, and we promote an ethos, not only of respect, understanding and appreciation of difference, but an ethos where difference in individuals and in groups is supported and celebrated. If you wish to join our team here in CCD we are seeking applications for a lecturer on our Degree in Business on the Introduction to Law Module. The ideal candidate should possess a passion for their subject and an ability to instil and encourage the same in their students. Teaching experience is a preference for this position which is available on a part\-time basis. **Details:** **Location:** Live online and In\-Person (depending on student demand) **Duration:** 10 weeks **Day \& Times:** Monday \- 6:30pm \- 9:30pm **Start Date:** 18th May 2026 **Syllabus:** * Introduction to Immigration Law * Entry and refusal at borders and Initial residence * Residence in the state * Free movement of presons * Employment Permits and Corporate Immigration * International Protection * Citizenship and Naturalisation * Deportation from the State **Required Education, Skills and Qualifications:** * Minimum post graduate qualification in Law is essential * Relevant and subject related industry experience * Previous experience teahcing or delivering training is desired * Ability to communicate effectively both orally and in writing * Excellent Presentation, I.T. and Interpersonal skills * Proactive approach to supporting and developing others with a demonstrate\-able interest in learner employability, activation, and progression

title: Implementation Consultant (SaaS, €75k)
description: If you thrive on delivering complex, technical solutions, enjoy building things from scratch, and are ready to take ownership of post\-sale success for enterprise clients \- this is your next challenge. You're applying for a fast\-growing, globally trusted software (Series D, $100m ARR) company helping large organisations implement critical, highly customised solutions who are looking for a **Senior Implementation Consultant** to lead end\-to\-end delivery for strategic enterprise clients You'll be working directly with senior stakeholders and cross\-functional teams. It's a **hands\-on, high\-impact post\-sale delivery** , where your technical expertise, problem\-solving, and ability to thrive in ambiguity will drive success. **What You’ll Own:** * Lead **end\-to\-end post\-sale implementations** for enterprise clients. * Manage timelines, risks, and escalations while delivering **highly customised solutions** . * Collaborate closely with Engineering, Product, Account Management, and Customer Success teams. * Translate complex customer requirements into **practical, technical implementations** . * Resolve complex technical and delivery issues, ensuring smooth, timely outcomes. * Contribute to and improve implementation methodologies and best practices. * Mentor and support junior implementation team members. **What You Bring:** * 5\+ years implementing software for **enterprise or strategic customers** . * Proven track record delivering **complex, technical, post\-sale implementations** . * Direct customer\-facing experience with **large global organisations** (e.g., Booking.com, WorldPay). * Strong experience with **APIs (RESTful)** , JSON, JavaScript, SQL, and relational databases. * Comfortable managing multiple implementations in **ambiguous, first\-time delivery scenarios** . * Strong problem\-solving skills and **technical acumen** . * Degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Software, or related field preferred; formal PM certification is a plus but not essential. * Experience in SME or scale\-up environments preferred \- **mindset matters more than brand** . **The good stuff:** * **Location:** Dublin (Hybrid – Tues/Wed/Thurs in office) * **Type:** Full\-time, permanent * **Salary:** Up to €75k \& benefits If you've read this far, it means there is clearly a level of interest and you should apply (If you are the right person of course!) If you want to **own enterprise post\-sale delivery** , work with complex systems, and make an impact from day one: APPLY NOW!

title: International Marketing Intern (Un-paid)
description: **About Napblog Limited** Napblog Limited is an Irish R\&D company building innovative products and services at the intersection of technology, AI, and creative industries. We operate with a blended modern Irish work culture — collaborative, ideas\-first, and globally minded — and are proud to work alongside a diverse international community of experts. **Role: International Marketing Intern (Un\-paid)** Remote \| 4 hours per week commitment **About the Role** This is a rare opportunity to get hands\-on experience operationalising international marketing strategies for cutting\-edge R\&D products and services. You'll work directly with the Founder, contributing to Napblog's global marketing efforts through creative brainstorming and the implementation of AI\-powered automations to bring ideas to life. Most of your time will be spent co\-creating strategies, experimenting with AI tools, and tapping into Napblog's diverse international marketing expert community. This is ideal for anyone who wants real, portfolio\-worthy experience in a forward\-thinking, purpose\-driven environment. **What You'll Do** * Operationalise international marketing strategies for Napblog's R\&D products and services * Participate in brainstorming sessions with the Founder to develop and refine global marketing approaches * Implement AI automations to execute marketing campaigns and tasks efficiently * Collaborate with a diverse international community of marketing experts and creatives * Support the development of content, messaging, and outreach across international markets * Contribute ideas for expanding Napblog's global brand visibility **What We're Looking For** * A genuine passion for marketing, innovation, and technology * Curiosity and enthusiasm for AI tools, automation, and creative problem\-solving * Strong communication skills with an open, collaborative mindset * Comfortable working independently in a flexible, remote setting * Interest in international markets and cross\-cultural communication * Background in marketing, business, communications, or a related field is a plus — but not required **What You Get in Return** While this is an un\-paid role, the experience and value you receive are substantial: * Professional job recommendation and referral letters upon completion * 1:1 mentoring directly from the Founder * A strong, real\-world international marketing portfolio * Cross\-domain creative learning experience across AI, technology, and Irish innovation culture * Exposure to a blended modern Irish work culture and a vibrant international expert community * A meaningful springboard for a career in international marketing, AI, or the tech industry This role is open to students, recent graduates, career changers, or anyone who wants to build meaningful global marketing experience with a purpose\-driven R\&D company.

title: Investment Data Analyst
description: Investment Data Analyst Location: Dublin Role Type: Permanent Work Setup: Hybrid **Who We Are** Vanguard is one of the world's leading investment firms, dedicated to helping clients achieve lasting financial success. Established in 1975, its unique ownership structure\-where funds own the company and investors own the funds\-ensures all efforts are focused on client outcomes. Known for integrity, innovation, and low\-cost investing, fosters an inclusive and collaborative culture that empowers employees to make a meaningful impact globally. **What You'll Do:** \* Deliver accurate, timely gold\-copy investment data for assigned domains, focusing on operational efficiency and quality. Handle complex tasks under tight deadlines, balancing multiple priorities, and liaise with internal and external partners to resolve data issues. Maintain system and business controls to ensure data integrity and authorised distribution. \* Execute and analyse daily and periodic data reports and quality checks accurately. Troubleshoot and resolve issues in reports or source systems, ensuring data feeds are timely and complete. Escalate unresolved issues and provide reporting for regulatory and internal requirements. \* Identify process improvement opportunities, perform root cause analysis of data errors, and implement short\- and long\-term solutions. Escalate issues as appropriate. \* Build strong networks within Data Management and collaborate across FFS, IMG, and the wider enterprise to enhance global operational processes. \* Develop deep knowledge of assigned funds and securities, understanding operational processes and downstream impacts, to support gold\-copy data production. \* Supports projects through testing, system monitoring, and related tasks. Ensures data quality by creating test cases and reports to verify accuracy and completeness. \* Maintain and improve Data Management procedures and workflows, promptly escalating potential control gaps. Ensure adherence to control standards for compliance. \* Contribute to special projects and perform other duties as required. **What You Bring:** \* Undergraduate degree in a relevant field or equivalent experience. \* At least two years' relevant experience, including ideally one year in the financial or investment sector. \* Experience in investment data management, process improvement, and financial services operations (2\+ years). \* Proficient in SQL and/or Python. \* Experience in project coordination. **What's Next:** If you are ready to take the next step, apply now. Successful applicants will be contacted directly by a recruiter to discuss the role more. We are committed to creating an inclusive recruitment experience. If you require support or adjustments to the recruitment process, our Adjustment Concierge Service is here to help. Please feel free to contact us at **adjustments@robertwalters.com** to discuss how we can support you. *This position is being recruited on behalf of our client through our Outsourcing service line. Resource Solutions Limited, trading as Robert Walters, acts as an employment business and agency, partnering with top organizations to help them find the best talent. We welcome applications from all candidates and are committed to providing equal opportunities.*

title: Java Developer
description: **Company Description** Version 1 has celebrated 30 years in business and continues to be trusted by global brands to deliver technology and transformation solutions that drive customer success. Our deep expertise enables our customers to navigate the rapidly evolving technology landscape. We foster strong partnerships with global technology leaders including Microsoft, AWS, Oracle, Red Hat, OutSystems, Snowflake, ensuring that our customers are provided with the highest quality solutions and services. We’re an award\-winning employer reflecting how our employees are at the very heart of what we do: * UK \& Ireland's premier AWS, Microsoft \& Oracle partner * 3300\+ strong, €350/£300m revenue business * 10\+ years as a Great Place to Work in Ireland \& UK * Best Workplace for Women in the UK \& Ireland by GPTW * Best Workplace for Wellbeing in the UK by GPTW We’re a core valuesdriven company, we hire people who share our values, and we reward those who display and foster them, it’s deeply embedded within our DNA. Invest in us and we’ll invest in you. **Job Description** Job Description: We are seeking a highly skilled and experienced Java Developer to join our dynamic team. The ideal candidate will have a strong background in Java development, extensive experience with AWS, database management, and preferred work experience in batch processing. As a Java Developer, you will play a crucial role in designing, developing, and maintaining high\-performance applications that meet our business needs. Key Responsibilities: * Design and Development: Design and development of robust, scalable, and efficient Java applications. * AWS Integration: Utilize AWS services to build and deploy cloud\-based solutions, ensuring high availability and scalability. * Database Management: Design, implement, and maintain database schemas, write complex SQL queries, and optimize database performance. * Batch Processing: Develop and manage batch processing systems to handle large volumes of data efficiently. * Code Quality: Ensure code quality through code reviews, unit testing, and adherence to best practices. * Collaboration: Work closely with cross\-functional teams, including product managers, QA engineers, and other developers, to deliver high\-quality software solutions. * Troubleshooting: Identify and resolve performance bottlenecks, bugs, and other technical issues. * Mentorship: Provide guidance and mentorship to junior developers, fostering a culture of continuous learning and improvement. **Qualifications** * Education: Bachelor’s or master’s degree in computer science, Engineering, or a related field. * Experience: Proven experience of 5\+ years working as a Java Developer in product development or services environment. * AWS Skills: Minimum 3\+ years of work experience in AWS services such as EC2, S3, Lambda function, Step\-functions, Event bus etc. * Database Skills: Minimum 5\+ years of work experience with relational databases (e.g., Oracle, MySQL, PostgreSQL), ability to write complex joins, performance troubleshooting experience etc. * Technical Skills: Proficiency in Java, Spring Framework, Hibernate, and RESTful APIs. * Problem\-Solving: Excellent analytical and problem\-solving skills. * Communication: Strong verbal and written communication skills. * Team Player: Ability to work effectively in a collaborative team environment. Preferred Qualifications: * Batch Processing experience: Hands\-on experience with batch processing frameworks and tools. * Python experience: Minimum of 2 years is nice to have. **Additional Information** **Why Version 1?** At Version 1, we believe in providing our employees with a comprehensive benefits package that prioritises their wellbeing, professional growth, and financial stability. * Share in our success with our Quarterly Performance\-Related Profit Share Scheme, where employees collectively benefit from a share of our company's profits * Strong Career Progression \& mentorship coaching through our Strength in Balance \& Leadership schemes with a dedicated quarterly Pathways Career Development programme * Flexible/remote working, Version 1 is tremendously understanding of life events and people’s individual circumstances and offer flexibility to help achieve a healthy work life balance * Financial Wellbeing initiatives including; Pension, Private Healthcare Cover, Life Assurance, Financial advice and an Employee Discount scheme * Employee Wellbeing schemes including Gym Discounts, Bike to Work, Fitness classes, Mindfulness Workshops, Employee Assistance Programme and much more. Generous holiday allowance, enhanced maternity/paternity leave, marriage/civil partnership leave and special leave policies * Educational assistance, incentivised certifications, and accreditations, including AWS, Microsoft, Oracle, and Red Hat * Reward schemes including Version 1’s Annual Excellence Awards \& ‘Call\-Out’ platform. * Environment, Social and Community First initiatives allow you to get involved in local fundraising and development opportunities as part of fostering our diversity, inclusion and belonging schemes. And many more exciting benefits… drop us a note to find out more. Version 1 is an equal opportunities employer. We are committed to building a diverse, inclusive and respectful workplace where everyone feels valued and able to thrive. We welcome applications from people of all backgrounds, identities and lived experiences, and we value the different perspectives people bring. We want every candidate to have a positive and accessible recruitment experience. If you need reasonable adjustments at any stage of the process, please contact ekta.bahl@version1\.com at Version 1\. We will consider all requests carefully, respectfully and confidentially. Video links: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v\=F\_d3ELTH5zo \#LI\-EB1

title: Junior Electrical Engineer
description: **Junior Electrical Engineer** *TDE Group – Kildare* **About Us:** TDE Group is a dynamic and growing electrical contracting company delivering projects across domestic, commercial, and industrial sectors. With a strong focus on quality, safety, and client satisfaction, we are expanding our project management and engineering team to support larger scale projects. **The Role:** We are seeking a motivated **Junior Electrical Engineer** to join our team. This is an excellent opportunity for someone looking to develop their career in the electrical contracting industry, gaining exposure to both office and site\-based work. **Key Responsibilities:** * Prepare and update **CAD drawings**, including design, and handover drawings. * Assist in the preparation of **Operation \& Maintenance (O\&M) manuals**. * Support the project team with **tender packages**, including gathering quotations and compiling documents. * Carry out **general administrative tasks** related to project delivery. * Provide technical support to project managers. * Attend site visits and assist in preparing reports and progress documentation. * Liaise with suppliers, subcontractors, and clients when required. **Requirements:** * Degree / Diploma in Electrical Engineering or similar. * Knowledge of **AutoCAD or similar drawing software**. * Strong attention to detail and organisational skills. * Ability to work both independently and as part of a team. * Excellent communication skills. * Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, PowerPoint). * Full clean driver’s licence desirable. **What We Offer:** * Competitive salary (DOE). * Mentorship and support. * Exposure to a wide range of electrical projects across multiple sectors. * Career progression opportunities within a rapidly growing company. * Support for further training and professional development. title: Junior Health And Safety Officer
description: We are seeking a Dublin based Junior Health \& Safety Officer to join the safety department. Reporting to the Health \& Safety Manager, requirements and responsibilities shall include: **Key Requirements:** * 3rd Level Qualification in Health \& Safety (Minimum Diploma). * 2 years post qualification experience in a similar role. * Ability to work and communicate confidently and effectively with both senior management within company as well as external clients and contractors. * Experience with relevant HSQE standards and management systems. * Proficiency in carrying out safety inspections. * A genuine passion for H\&S matters, with a record of making measurable improvements to operational practices. * Previous data centre experience is not essential but is advantageous. **Key Responsibilities:** * Carrying out safety inspections and producing risk assessments. * Regularly review and update Company Risk Assessments, Safe Operating Procedures and Safety Statement. * Complete Safety Induction Training for all new employees and sub\-contractors, and maintain documentary evidence of same. * Investigate, record and report accidents, incidents and near\-misses promptly, ensuring any corrective actions are implemented without delay. * Provide relevant accident information for insurance purposes. * Identify new and on\-going safety related training requirements, ensuring all mandatory training is reviewed and delivered on time. * Ensure that correct PPE is availed of at all times by all relevant employees. * Enforce Health and Safety Department Policies. * Review sub\-contractors’ Safety Statements and Safety Compliance. * Attend regular Health \& Safety meetings. Strong time management and communication skills are essential to this role as well as computer literacy and the ability to prepare reports and administer necessary paperwork.

title: Junior Site Engineer
description: **Junior Site Engineer – Civil Infrastructure** A leading civil engineering contractor is seeking a **Graduate / Junior Site Engineer** to join their growing team across projects in **Meath** and surrounding areas. title: Legal Administrator
description: We are representing one of Ireland’s premier commercial law firms in their search for a skilled Legal Administrator to join their dedicated New Homes team. This is an excellent opportunity to join a growing, award\-winning firm that provides "top\-tier" legal services both in Ireland and internationally. **The Role:** Working closely with property specialists, you will support all stages of new\-build transactions in a fast\-paced, client\-focused environment. Your focus will be on ensuring a smooth day\-to\-day workflow through meticulous documentation management and clear communication. **Key Responsibilities:** * **Matter Management:** Setting up new client matters in Keyhouse, ensuring data accuracy and compliance. * **Financial Support:** Assisting with drafting, reviewing, and finalizing invoices for timely issuance. * **Document Control:** Managing team post, scanning, and saving contract/closing documents to iManage with correct naming standards. * **Closing Support:** Compiling organized and complete contract and closing packs to support client completions. * **Liaison:** Effectively communicating with developers and purchasers' solicitors throughout the transaction lifecycle. **The Successful Candidate:** * **Organized \& Efficient:** Able to manage multiple tasks with strong attention to detail and accuracy. * **System Savvy:** Comfortable using legal systems such as **Keyhouse** and **iManage** (training can be provided). * **Team Player:** Communicates clearly and maintains a proactive, professional approach within a busy team. * **Confidentiality:** High level of professionalism regarding sensitive client information. **Why Apply?** This firm offers a collaborative and inclusive culture with a genuine focus on your professional development and career progression. **Comprehensive Benefits Include:** * Access to associate and partner development programs. * Active firm committees (Social, Wellness, Green, Diversity, and Pro Bono). * On\-site gym and subsidised yoga/fitness classes. * A vibrant social calendar with numerous events throughout the year.

title: Level 1 Onsite Support Agent
description: My Dublin based client is recruiting for a Junior Level 1 Onsite Support agent to support a hardware refresh across approximately 350 desks. The role involves replacing existing Dell docking stations / OptiPlex devices with Lenovo docking stations. This is a straightforward, repetitive task requiring basic technical handling and strong attention to detail. **This is a short term daily rate contract onsite and with an immediate start.** **Responsibilities** * Remove existing Dell docking stations / OptiPlex devices * Install and connect Lenovo docking stations * Disconnect and reconnect 3–4 cables per desk (power, monitor, USB, network) * Ensure each workstation is left fully functional * Record key asset details: * Desk number * Serial number of removed device(s) * Installed docking station details * Escalate any issues encountered onsite **Requirements** * Basic IT hardware knowledge * Strong attention to detail * Ability to follow instructions and repeat tasks accurately * Comfortable working onsite and independently **Deliverables** * All 350 desks successfully updated * Accurate asset tracking log completed * No disruption to end\-user setups

title: Life and Pensions Administrator
description: **🏢 Job Title:** Life and Pensions Administrator **📍 Location:** Dublin, Ireland (Onsite) **🕒 Employment Type:** Full\-Time \| Permanent **💶 Salary:** €35,000 \- €50,000 (depending on experience) **📅 Start Date:** Immediately **About** A leading financial services firm is seeking an experienced administrator to join their expanding team. title: Maintenance Technician
description: **Background:** Veolia is Ireland’s leading environmental services company. We provide a comprehensive range of energy, waste and water solutions and are dedicated to carbon reduction, protecting the environment and building the circular economy. We offer all employees a benefits package as standard which includes full VHI cover, pension and education assistance. When you see the world as we do, you see the chance to help the world take better care of its resources, and help it become a better place for everyone. It's why we're looking for someone who's just as committed as we are, to push for genuine change and bring our ambition of Ecological Transformation to life. We know that everyone here at Veolia can help us work alongside our communities, look after the environment, and contribute to our inclusive culture. **Maintenance Technician** **Location:** **Grangecastle, Dublin** **Duration:** **Permanent** **Hours:** **39 hours per week (08:00 to 16:30\)** **Overview of the Role:** The Maintenance Technician is responsible for the execution of the Preventive and Corrective maintenance program as per the Client / PPU requirement. **Day to Day of the Role:** * Day\-to\-Day Relationship with the Veolia Site Manager, Client, Shift Lead and Engineering team. * Maintaining and building new relationships across the site among the internal Veolia team, Client, Engineering team and sub\-contractors * Point of contact for escalation of any issues w.r.t. Plant maintenance. * Troubleshoot problems with utilities equipment as necessary and as directed by site supervisors * Complete site documentation before and after work tasks, as appropriate * Update MTL’s as appropriate * All work on site to be carried out in accordance with site EHS (Environmental Health and Safety) and Veolia EHS rules and guidelines. * Liaise and Work with the Shift team on all Utilities Equipment * Liaise with Contractors during planned service maintenance. * Participate in the appropriate training. * Carry out Risk Assessment of Equipment Maintenance Task List steps and ensure these are always kept valid. (PMO) * Carry out Periodic reviews of EWI/SOP to ensure documents are always current and valid. * Trouble\-shooting and investigation of utility plant equipment faults and failures along with the Shift Operation Group. * Identifying, liaising with, and, where appropriate, co\-ordination of third\-party contractors and suppliers on site. * Ensuring Engineering areas, in liaison with Contract Manager and Contract co\-ordinator, facilities and equipment are kept in a clean, safe and serviceable condition, and general housekeeping highest standards are maintained. * Continuous improvement of contract delivery on site **Required Skills for the Role:** * Senior Trades, City \& Guilds or equivalent in Mechanical / Building Services engineering. * Engineering degree/diploma or equivalent, preferably mechanical/environmental, with two to three years post graduate experience, preferably in mechanical/building services, or * Marine qualification, with 2 to 4 years’ experience at sea. (Desirable but not necessary) **Essential:** * Mechanical / Building Services / Utilities experience, 2 years minimum and good understanding of the basic fundamentals of the various utilities systems. * Pumps, HVAC Systems, Air Compressors, Boilers, Plant steam systems and ancillary services, (DHW /DCW /LPHW skids), Chillers, Cooling Towers, Clean steam Boilers, Purified Water Systems, WFI Water Systems, CIP systems, Refrigeration Systems, Process gases, * Building Management Systems (Scada) * Good understanding of Preventive Maintenance / Corrective Maintenance Systems. * Familiarity with Predictive Maintenance (CBM) techniques and their benefits. * Worked within a Computerized Maintenance Management (CMMS) Work Order system environment. (EAMS/SAP) * Work within a documented Permit to Work System, Method Statement and Risk Assessment and COHE methods. * Computer literate and good organizational skills * Ability to read and understand P\&ID’s. * Understanding of SOP’s/EWI’s and GMP Engineering systems. * Understanding of Basic Instrumentation * Must be able to demonstrate hands on experience with detailed knowledge/skill in the following area’s: * Troubleshooting / repair of Mechanical/Building Services systems * Good Initiative and Pro\-active attitude in approach to duties * Self\-motivation, ability to work alone and also as Team Player. * Attention to detail is critical * Methodical \& Analytical approach to work and the ability to communicate to Supervisor and Colleagues * Good problem solving and trouble shooting ability. * Good communication and interpersonal skills * Computer Skills (Excel, Word, Client PC Applications etc) **Desirable:** * Experience in the pharmaceutical, Biotech or food industry * Recertification of bio\-Safety cabinets / Fume hoods. Autoclaves. * Analytical laboratory environment experience * Maintenance and troubleshooting experience of the following systems and processes – RO/WFI, Autoclaves, fume\-hoods, bio\-safety cabinets, LAF cabinets; Fire suppression systems; Vacuum. **Please note:** If you are an EU/EEA national, you will be asked to show proof of right to work in Ireland. If you are a non\-EU/EEA national , you will require current and valid permission to work and reside in the Republic of Ireland. Information on legislation and guides to the procedures in relation to obtaining greencard permits ,work permits, spousal/dependant permits is available on the Department of Jobs, Enterprise and Innovation website.

title: O2C Collections Analyst (Italian)
description: **Location: Dublin 4** **ROLE RESPONSIBILITIES** These responsibilities include but are not limited to the following: * Proactively analyze AR falling due and delinquent account balances to ensure timely collection of 3rd party receivables. * Timely execution of customer dunning activities (e.g. outbound calls, emails, letters, etc.) * Coordinate customer account resolution with internal parties and execute agreed collection strategies. * Timely resolution of inbound billing inquiries (call, emails, letters, etc.) with respective parties. * Maintenance of subledger. deadlines. * Manage and resolve escalations in a timely manner, ensuring appropriate management oversight where necessary. * Manage time effectively and communicate with peers and management with regards to work tasks, status and deadlines. * Proactively ensure hand\-offs to supporting teams are timely and clearly communicated as part of a virtual global Collections team i.e. Other regional hubs for Collections, Cash application, Credit Risk COE, G2N etc. * Maintain receipt records, files and documentation in an organized manner. * Share audit documentation to support periodic SAP PC control performance and internal/external audit programs. * Participate in F\&GBS Order\-to\-Cash global initiatives including transformational projects as needed, as a subject matter expert for the area. * Ensure strong commitment to the control environment through training and review to meet all control and compliance requirements. **QUALIFICATIONS** **Education and Experience** * Business English and Native Italian Language requirements to support effective engagement with Customers and colleagues/business partners as needed (fluent/native verbal and written Native Italian language skills required). * Degree in Finance or Economics or Accounting preferred. * Experience in Accounts receivable and/or broader Order to Cash function required * Ability to work in a high\-performing team environment. * Strong interpersonal skills and ability to communicate with diverse groups and individuals. * Demonstrated ability to work on own initiative. * Strong verbal and written communication skills. * Experience of working in cross\-functional and multi\-cultural team an advantage. * Strong business acumen and professionalism with the ability to interface effectively with all levels of management and operations colleagues in all applicable Pfizer Finance and business functional areas * Experience with financial shared services preferred. **Technical Skills Requirements** * Proficiency in PC skills (MS Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Teams) required * SAP ERP system experience preferred including FSCM Collections and Dispute Mgt.

title: Operations Associate (FTC) - Grafton Street
description: **WHY JOIN ALO?** Mindful movement. It’s at the core of why we do what we do at ALO—it’s our calling. Because mindful movement in the studio leads to better living. It changes who yogis are off the mat, making their lives and their communities better. That’s the real meaning of studio\-to\-street: taking the consciousness from practice on the mat and putting it into practice in life. **Objective** The Operations Associate is critical in the store team and is responsible for driving to profitability and efficiency. The Operations Associate will work closely with the Operations \& Visual Manager \& Operations Lead in initiating, completing work, and providing feedback to the team to achieve optimal results supporting total store operations. **This is a 6\-month, fixed\-term contract.** **Key Job Responsibilities** * Execute inventory management processes effectively with a focus on maximizing inventory accuracy and reducing shrink * Investigate and root cause inventory accuracy issues, partnering with the General Manager to escalate process or system gaps * Support execution of key operational functions including shipment, receiving and processing, reverse transfers, sales floor recovery, intra\-day sales floor replenishment, floor set updates, and supply management * Maintain and champion strong visual standards for the sales floor * Work closely with reporting, budgets, expenses, inventory control, supplies, vendor relationships, technology, and facilities to ensure maintained and maximized * Support a safe work environment and efficient operation through strong stockroom standards and processes * Move dynamically on the retail floor to assess and fulfill the needs of the business, team and guests * Execute company\-level operations\-focused directives, projects and initiates, in partnership with the Operations \& Visual Manager \& Operations Lead. * Assists in processing shipment and ensuring product flow; including but not limited to visual merchandising placement and daily replenishment excellence * Demonstrate strong business acumen by leveraging metrics to support business\-driving strategies * Resolve client needs quickly \& effectively ensuring customer satisfaction * Identify product concerns and communicate inventory needs to support the business goals * Support other areas of the business, including Sales \& Service and Cashier, as needed **Operations Associate Qualifications** * Preferred prior work experience in a client\-centric, sales \& operational environment * Passion for customer service and delivering exceptional experiences * High energy, upbeat and enthusiastic with the ability to integrate fun and work * Self\-motivated with a desire to achieve results and excel individually, and as a team * Fosters genuine connection through empathy, compassion, integrity and building trusting relationships * Aligns with and embodies ALO's Guiding Principles * Proof of right to live and work in the UK **Operations Associate Schedule** Part\-Time associates are expected to work up to 29 hours per week; Full\-Time associates are expected to work 32\-40 hours per week with 100% of their work week in operations and floor support. All employees' availability must suit the needs of the business, which are subject to change. The General Manager determines availability needs and creates a schedule including morning, evening, and weekend shifts, which may be subject to change. All Employees are expected to be able to work from the week of and including “Black Friday” until the end of the holiday season, first week of January. **Diversity and Inclusion statement** As an Equal Opportunity Employer, ALO does not discriminate against applicants or employees because of race, colour, creed, religion, sex, national origin, veteran status, disability, age, citizenship, marital or domestic/civil partnership status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression or because of any other status or condition protected by statutory law. We are committed to an active Inclusion, Diversity and Equal Opportunities Policy, which starts with our recruitment and selection process, and we are happy to talk flexible working. **Interview Process adjustments** If you consider yourself to require reasonable adjustments to any part of our recruitment process, we invite you to share those requirements with us when completing your application or by emailing europehr@aloyoga.com. We will make every effort to ensure your needs are met to provide a fair and transparent process of assessment. For CA residents, Job Applicant Privacy Policy HERE.
